metadata.title: Python Code Snippets - Functions
metadata.description: Copy-ready Python code examples for Week 1
metadata.usage: Each snippet can be copied directly into Jupyter Notebook

snippets.title: Standard Imports for Week 1
snippets.description: Import statements for Functions.ipynb and PlasticPollution.ipynb
snippets.code:
%matplotlib inline
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import math

snippets.title: Defining a Linear Function
snippets.description: General linear function y = ax + b
snippets.code:
def linear_fun(a, b, x):
    y = a * x + b
    return y

# Example: y = 2x + 5
xvals = np.linspace(-4, 4, 9)
y1vals = linear_fun(a=2, b=5, x=xvals)
print(y1vals)

snippets.title: Defining a Quadratic Function
snippets.description: General quadratic function y = ax² + bx + c
snippets.code:
def quad_fun(x, a, b, c):
    y = a * (x**2) + b * x + c
    return y

# Example: y = x² - 4x + 3
xvals = np.linspace(-2, 6, 17)
yvals = quad_fun(x=xvals, a=1, b=-4, c=3)
print(yvals)

snippets.title: Piecewise Function with if-else
snippets.description: Function with different rules for different domains

snippets.title: Reisser Depth-Integration Model
snippets.description: Calculates the correction ratio for ocean plastic sampling
snippets.code:
def CiCsRatio_fun(d, alpha=0.4):
    """Calculate depth-integration correction ratio.
    
    Args:
        d: immersion depth in metres (must be > 0)
        alpha: parameter (default 0.4 m^-1)
    
    Returns:
        Correction ratio Ci/Cs
    """
    ratio = 1 / (1 - np.exp(-alpha * d))
    return ratio

# Calculate for different depths
d_values = np.array([0.1, 0.17, 0.3, 1.0])
for d in d_values:
    print(f"R({d}) = {CiCsRatio_fun(d):.2f}")

snippets.title: Plotting the Reisser Model
snippets.description: Visualize how correction ratio changes with depth
snippets.code:
# Create depth values (avoid d=0 where function is undefined)
d = np.linspace(0.05, 2.0, 40)

# Calculate correction ratios
cdf = pd.DataFrame()
cdf['d'] = d
cdf['CiCsRatio'] = CiCsRatio_fun(d)

# Plot
cdf.plot(x='d', y='CiCsRatio', 
         title='Correction Ratio vs Sampling Depth',
         xlabel='Depth (m)',
         ylabel='Ci/Cs Ratio',
         legend=False,
         grid=True)
plt.show()

snippets.title: Loading Global Plastics Data
snippets.description: Read and explore the production dataset
snippets.code:
# Read the CSV file from GitHub (works on Google Colab too)
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ahailu95/scie1500-content/main/SCIE1500Materials/Week_1/LabFiles/global-plastics-production.csv"
gpp = pd.read_csv(url)

# Explore the data
print(gpp.info())
print(gpp.head())
print(gpp.tail())

snippets.title: Creating Derived Variables
snippets.description: Add time trend and convert units
snippets.code:
# Create time trend (t = 1 for 1950)
gpp['t'] = gpp['Year'] - 1949

# Convert to million metric tonnes
gpp['GPP (MMT)'] = gpp['GPP (MT)'] / 1_000_000

# Check results
print(gpp[['Year', 't', 'GPP (MT)', 'GPP (MMT)']].head())

snippets.title: Plotting Global Plastic Production
snippets.description: Time series plot of production data
snippets.code:
gpp.plot(x='Year', 
         y='GPP (MMT)',
         title='Global Plastic Production (1950-2015)',
         xlabel='Year',
         ylabel='Production (Million Metric Tonnes)',
         kind='line',
         grid=True,
         color='red',
         legend=False)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

snippets.title: Plotting Multiple Linear Functions
snippets.description: Compare different linear equations on one chart
snippets.code:
# Create x values
xvals = np.linspace(-4, 4, 9)

# Calculate y values for different equations
y1vals = linear_fun(a=2, b=5, x=xvals)   # y = 2x + 5
y2vals = linear_fun(a=2, b=0, x=xvals)   # y = 2x
y3vals = linear_fun(a=-3, b=4, x=xvals)  # y = -3x + 4

# Create DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame()
df['x'] = xvals
df['y = 2x + 5'] = y1vals
df['y = 2x'] = y2vals
df['y = -3x + 4'] = y3vals

# Plot all three
df.plot(x='x', 
        y=['y = 2x + 5', 'y = 2x', 'y = -3x + 4'],
        title='Plot of Three Linear Equations',
        xlabel='x',
        ylabel='y',
        kind='line',
        grid=True,
        legend=True)
plt.show()

snippets.title: Plotting Quadratic Functions
snippets.code:
# Create x values
xvals = np.linspace(-10, 10, 25)

# Create DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame()
df['x'] = xvals

# Calculate y values for different quadratics
df['y1'] = quad_fun(x=xvals, a=1, b=-1, c=-2)    # Opens up
df['y2'] = quad_fun(x=xvals, a=-2, b=0, c=150)   # Opens down
df['y3'] = quad_fun(x=xvals, a=3, b=2, c=-50)    # Opens up

# Plot
df.plot(x='x', 
        y=['y1', 'y2', 'y3'],
        title='Comparison of Quadratic Functions',
        xlabel='x',
        ylabel='y',
        kind='line',
        grid=True,
        legend=True)
plt.show()

snippets.title: Finding Quadratic Vertex
snippets.code:
def find_vertex(a, b, c):
    """Find vertex of quadratic y = ax² + bx + c
    
    Returns:
        (x_vertex, y_vertex)
    """
    x_v = -b / (2 * a)
    y_v = a * x_v**2 + b * x_v + c
    return (x_v, y_v)

# Example: E(t) = -0.5t² + 4t + 2
a, b, c = -0.5, 4, 2
vertex = find_vertex(a, b, c)
print(f"Vertex: ({vertex[0]}, {vertex[1]})")
